title: Legislation especially important in light of recent cases connecting Roundup to cancer

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Glyphosate, the active chemical in the weed killer Roundup, has been cited in recent court cases for causing cancer.",
    "A judge ordered Roundup's parent company Bayer AG to pay more than $2 billion in damages to a California family that claimed they got cancer from using Roundup for several decades.",
    "There is growing evidence that glyphosate causes cancer.",
    "According to a California Department of Pesticide Regulation (DPR) report, glyphosate is a probable carcinogen.",
    "The three most frequently used pesticides on California school sites are glyphosate, strychnine, and bifenthrin.",
    "58% of pesticide applications occurred outdoors on California school sites, with roughly 75% occurring during school days.",
    "According to recent data, 178 different active pesticide ingredients were used in California schools in 2015.",
    "Of the 30 most commonly used lawn pesticides, 16 are possible and/or known carcinogens.",
    "17 of the most commonly used lawn pesticides have the potential to disrupt the endocrine (hormonal) system.",
    "21 of the most commonly used lawn pesticides are linked to reproductive effects and sexual dysfunction.",
    "12 of the most commonly used lawn pesticides have been linked to birth defects.",
    "14 of the most commonly used lawn pesticides are neurotoxic.",
    "25 of the most commonly used lawn pesticides can cause kidney or liver damage.",
    "26 of the most commonly used lawn pesticides are sensitizers and/or irritants."
  ]
}
```
